Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} ``Ooyatsuhime Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}} Udamori. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined God ===
'''Abbreviated genealogy of the three Ita-kei gods'''
The names of the gods are written in the Nihon Shoki, and the names of the shrines are written in the Engishiki. {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{jalink|Susano-o|Q272993|Susa no Takashi}}
({{jalink|Susa Shrine (Arita City)|Q11664501|Susa Shrine}})}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|+|-|-|.| }}{{familytree|border=0|01|02|03| 01={{jalink|50 Fierce God|Q10881556|50 Fierce Life}}
({{jalink|Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271}})|02='''{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}'''
('''Oyatsuhime Shrine''')|03={{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Himetsuhime no Mikoto}}}}
({{jalink|Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259}})}} {{familytree/end}}
'''Main enshrined deity''' * '''{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Ooyatsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Ooyatsuhime Mikoto) '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Wakimiya: {{jalink|Goju Takekami|Q10881556|Goju Takekami}} (Itakeru Mikoto) *: Oyatsuhime no Mikoto's older brother deity, {{jalink|Ita-Kiso Shrine|Q11379271}} enshrined deity. * Right side shrine: {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyatomatohime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto) *: The sister deity of Oyatsu Hime no Mikoto, and the enshrined deity {{jalink|Toumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259}}.As the company name suggests, our company is said to be a shrine dedicated to '''{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Ooyatsuhime}}''' (Oyatsuhime no Mikoto). Regarding Ooyatsuhime, in the 5th book of the ``{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'' Jindaiki's Treasure Sword Appearance Stage (8th Stage of the First Jindai Period), it is described that she is the daughter god of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo}} (Susanoo). Also, the older brother god of Ooyatsuhime is {{jalink|Isotakeru|Q10881556|Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the younger sister god is {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Sakutsuhime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsu Both gods are currently enshrined at {{jalink|Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{jalink|Tomatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259}} (3 Ronsha shrines), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree seeds to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itae Sosanjin'''' {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Furthermore, in the Jigihonki, ``{{jalink|Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751}}'', these three gods are said to be the gods enshrined by {{jalink|Kiokunizo|Q11604953}}.According to the shrine's legend, there was once a vast forest in the area around our company, and the wood was used to build houses, so it was given the divine name of ``Oya.'' According to "{{jalink|Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}", the name of the enshrined place, "Udamori", came from the company's shrine complex.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. === History === === Founding ===Founding date is unknown. Shrine biography"Shrine Furyu" ({{Harvtxt|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=46-47}}). According to .94|N|135|12|4.67|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|Location|name=Maezen Shrine/Kokake Shrine (first enshrined place?①)}}) It was originally enshrined in the ground, but in {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}, the site was given to Ryojingu Shrine in 1616 and renamed to the ``Boar Forest'' in Shandong (Itaikeso, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|1 1|58.42|N|135|15|14.38|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Boar Forest (former enshrined place?②)}})Currently, Sansei Shrine, a shrine outside the precincts of {{jalink|Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271}}, is located in Ino no Mori. {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. After that, "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}" written by {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} In the 2nd year ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}), the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oyatsuhime, and Tomatuhime were transferred from Ino-no-Mori to the old shrine north of the current shrine site (Kitano-no-miya, Wakayama City). Mount Hara, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|17.9|N|135|15|2.4|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Old palace (former enshrined place or ③)}}) There are currently no remains of the old palace. , and later relocated to its current location.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}In the Kii Famous Places Zue, ``Mt. Mihara'' is written as ``Mt. Miharayama'', and there is a different legend that it is the place where the three Ita-kami deities descended from the heavens with the seeds of wood, and from there they were divided into different shrines. .Also, "Oyatsu Hime-sha Engi" cited in "Zoku Fudoki" "Kii Zoku Fudoki". In , I will post a separate story that states that the three Itaige deities chose their respective places to be enshrined from Tenuki Bridge (bridge of the heavenly world) and went to various places {{jalink|Amakudari|Q480175|Amakudari}}, but Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto chose Hirata-go, descended there, ran a palace there, and was enshrined there. Although its relationship with our company is unknown, to the east of the shrine there is the Udamori Ruins, which is thought to be a {{jalink|ring-moat village|Q60995739|ring-moat village ruins}} from the middle of the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}}. === Outline === ==== Ancient ==== The first reference to the history of the country as a shrine is in the aforementioned article "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}" {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 2nd year ({{jalink|702|Q23497}})'Shoku Nihongi' article dated February 22, 702. In , it is said that the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime were separated."Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , seven {{jalink|Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|Kobe}} (private houses attached to the shrine) were granted to ``Oyatsuhime Shrine'' in Kii Province {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Related, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan) |Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}" is Kii Province {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} ``Oya-go'' is listed twice in , but one of these is ``Oya-Kobe'', which means our Kobe, and this Kobe is thought to have been distributed around Udamori. As {{jalink|Divine|Q11591025}}, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year of the year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), he was elevated to the rank of Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) along with Itagesojin and Tomatsuhimejin.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.{{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} |Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nagusa County|Q11415547}} is listed as "Oya Tsuhime Shrine" Meishin University It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Niinamatsuri|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q1 1501518}}, it is written that it was deposited in {{jalink|notebook|Q11481885}}{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. In ``{{jalink|Kinokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Oya Okami, the fourth rank of Jushii,'' but it is different from Itakeso Okami, who rose to Shoichii (Senior First Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.Company biography, in April of {{jalink|Kanji|Q1193874}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1088|Q19463}}), on the occasion of {{jalink|Emperor Horikawa|Q464267}}'s visit to {{jalink|Kumano}}, {{jalink|hohei|Q11 445508}}, and in the first year of {{jalink|Choji|Q1150870}} ({{jalink|1104|Q12490}}), there were {{jalink|Kanda|Q10855080}} of 18{{jalink|machibu}} (approximately 22 hectares) and 5{{jalink|machi (Unit)#Unit of Length|Q1185752|Town}} There is a similar description in 'Ki Province Famous Places Zue' that it prospered due to the donation of {{jalink|shrine land}} on all four sides (approximately 30 hectares). However, there is no other record of Emperor Horikawa's visit to Kumano. . ==== After the Middle Ages ==== According to the shrine biography , it declined after suffering from the {{jalink|Oei|Q1193564}} uprising (first half of the 16th century) and the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} military fire ({{jalink|Kishū Conquest#Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack|Q10855721|Toyotomi Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack}}). According to "Kanbunki" cited in "{{jalink|Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}", in the early modern period it had two towns of Kanda (approximately 2 hectares). Although this Kanda existed until after the war, it is said to have disappeared due to {{jalink|agrarian reform|Q208128}} in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}21 ({{jalink|1946|Q18610}}). group="Note">However, in Kanbunki, it is written in the past tense, ``existed until recent times (omitted).'' . In addition, the same article mentions that the area around our company, approximately 500 {{jalink|stone (unit)|Q855048|stone}}, is called the ``Kami no Ki'' and is thought to be the remains of a vast shrine complex from ancient times, but the details are not clear. In "{{jalink|Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}," the company at the time was the birthplace deity of the two villages of Udamori and Kitano, but it was originally considered to have been the birthplace deity of the entire Hirata Manor to which these two villages belonged, and it is assumed that due to the separation of the Konishi, Nishitai, and Kita villages due to a conflict within the manor, only the two villages became the birthplace.In April of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} |Q134917284}}, and in 1880|Q7806}}, it was promoted to {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. Also, in 1911|Q2076}}, the Soja Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi in the northern part of Wakayama City was {{jalink|combined enshrined|Q11412524}} {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}} (described later). After the war, it belonged to {{jalink|Shinja Honcho|Q908077}}. === God level === * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from the 8th grade of the junior fifth rank to the 8th grade of the lower rank of lower ranks (Junior 4th rank and lower rank of 8th rank) (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Oya Tsuhime no Kami''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii? (``{{jalink|Kinokuni Shinto Mecho|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Oya Okami''. It is said to be an error in writing ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper.'' === About Soja Shrine, a joint shrine === Oyatsuhime Shrine is the Sosha Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi, Kita Aza, Wakayama City, which was enshrined in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}44 ({{jalink|1911|Q2076}}). This Soja Shrine is a shrine that was also called ``Soja Daimyojin'' in early modern times, but the deity it enshrines is not clear (in ``Ki Meisho Zue'' it is said that {{jalink|Nippon Takeru}} is enshrined) {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3 ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) "Nanki Shrine Record" and its subsequent "Ki Meisho Zue" indicate that the shrine name and enshrinement location is "Soja" in Kii. |Q1629088}} is close to the estimated location (present-day Fuchu, Wakayama City), so this Soja Shrine is believed to be {{jalink|Soja|Q1107129}} in Kii Province.{{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. On the other hand, ``Kii Zoku Fudoki'' denies this and states that the shrine that was enshrined in Nezuku Valley in Taisho Ochiai Village (present-day Ochiai, Wakayama City) was transferred from the Sōron of the Shōchu period {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. === Precincts === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine building]] As for the shrine building, the central main hall has an Oji-zukuri copper plate roof, {{jalink|facing worship|Q11415970}} is {{jalink|kara gable|Q977293|kenkara gable}}, and {{jal {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}} where {{jalink|Chigi}} and {{jalink|Katsuoki}} are placed in the ridge of ink|Shinsha}}. To keep out the fog, all four sides of the shrine are covered with wooden walls. The side shrines on the left and right are also small-sized {{jalink|Nagazuzukuri|Q210684}} with copper plate roofing and have chigi and bonito trees {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}}. However, the Chigi of the left wakimiya (Isotake no Mikoto) is a soto-kege, and the chigi of the right wakimiya (Miyatomatohime no Mikoto) is an inward-kege. === Setsusha === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|小箠]] '''Precincts end shrine''' * Small shrine '''Outside shrine''' * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitano, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|12.6|N|135|14|41.1|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Outside shrine: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine}}) === Ritual organization === === Shinto priest === The priesthood of Oyatsuhime Shrine was held for {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} ({{jalink|1573|Q6678}}-{{jalink|1592|Q6769 }}) It is said that the Tsuchiya clan ({{jalink|real surname|Q11520116}}: Fujiwara clan) has been hereditary since around 1987|1987|p=46-47}}. However, in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue," when it was compiled in the late Edo period, the Shinto priest was the Mori clan, who claimed to be a descendant of Oyahiko no Mikoto, and the current generation is the 83rd generation from Oyahiko no Mikoto. === Parishioners === Oyatsuhime Shrine is said to be the local deity of six districts, including Udamori, where it is enshrined, Hironishi, Kitano, Nango, Nishitai, and Kita. |Q11454763}} was formed, but currently it only remains in the Nagaho district. According to "Kii Zoku Fudoki", {{jalink|yabusame|Q1886157}}, {{jalink|sarugaku|Q1204318}} and {{jalink|dengaku|Q3022455}} were once held as festivals on June 1st and September 21st (both according to the lunar calendar). The same book also states that the priests of Itae-so Shrine serve during the festivals held on the last day of October and November 16th (both according to the lunar calendar), and that in October they serve food such as red bean rice, persimmon mochi, fish, and sake. It is said that a {{jalink|portable shrine|Q979940}} from Itaukeso Shrine was {{jalink|transferred|Q11561919}} to our company in November. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} ``Ooyatsuhime Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}} Udamori. {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill||lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God ===
'''Abbreviated genealogy of the three Ita-kei gods'''
The names of the gods are written in the Nihon Shoki, and the names of the shrines are written in the Engishiki. {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{ill|lt=Susano-o|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa no Takashi}}

'''Main enshrined deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Ooyatsuhime Mikoto) '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Wakimiya: {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami}} (Itakeru Mikoto) *: Oyatsuhime no Mikoto's older brother deity, {{ill|lt=Ita-Kiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} enshrined deity. * Right side shrine: {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Miyatomatohime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto) *: The sister deity of Oyatsu Hime no Mikoto, and the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Toumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}}.As the company name suggests, our company is said to be a shrine dedicated to '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime}}''' (Oyatsuhime no Mikoto). Regarding Ooyatsuhime, in the 5th book of the ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' Jindaiki's Treasure Sword Appearance Stage (8th Stage of the First Jindai Period), it is described that she is the daughter god of {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} (Susanoo). Also, the older brother god of Ooyatsuhime is {{ill|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the younger sister god is {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Sakutsuhime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsu Both gods are currently enshrined at {{ill|lt=Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Tomatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}} (3 Ronsha shrines), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree seeds to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itae Sosanjin'''' {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Furthermore, in the Jigihonki, ``{{ill|lt=Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}}'', these three gods are said to be the gods enshrined by {{ill|lt=Kiokunizo|Q11604953|WD=Q11604953}}.According to the shrine's legend, there was once a vast forest in the area around our company, and the wood was used to build houses, so it was given the divine name of ``Oya.'' According to "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}", the name of the enshrined place, "Udamori", came from the company's shrine complex.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. === History === === Founding ===Founding date is unknown. Shrine biography"Shrine Furyu" ({{Harvtxt|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=46-47}}). According to .94|N|135|12|4.67|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|Location|name=Maezen Shrine/Kokake Shrine (first enshrined place?①)}}) It was originally enshrined in the ground, but in {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, the site was given to Ryojingu Shrine in 1616 and renamed to the ``Boar Forest'' in Shandong (Itaikeso, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|1 1|58.42|N|135|15|14.38|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Boar Forest (former enshrined place?②)}})Currently, Sansei Shrine, a shrine outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}}, is located in Ino no Mori. {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. After that, "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" written by {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} In the 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oyatsuhime, and Tomatuhime were transferred from Ino-no-Mori to the old shrine north of the current shrine site (Kitano-no-miya, Wakayama City). Mount Hara, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|17.9|N|135|15|2.4|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Old palace (former enshrined place or ③)}}) There are currently no remains of the old palace. , and later relocated to its current location.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}In the Kii Famous Places Zue, ``Mt. Mihara'' is written as ``Mt. Miharayama'', and there is a different legend that it is the place where the three Ita-kami deities descended from the heavens with the seeds of wood, and from there they were divided into different shrines. .Also, "Oyatsu Hime-sha Engi" cited in "Zoku Fudoki" "Kii Zoku Fudoki". In , I will post a separate story that states that the three Itaige deities chose their respective places to be enshrined from Tenuki Bridge (bridge of the heavenly world) and went to various places {{ill|lt=Amakudari|Q480175|WD=Q480175|lt=Amakudari}}, but Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto chose Hirata-go, descended there, ran a palace there, and was enshrined there. Although its relationship with our company is unknown, to the east of the shrine there is the Udamori Ruins, which is thought to be a {{ill|lt=ring-moat village|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739|lt=ring-moat village ruins}} from the middle of the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}}. === Outline === ==== Ancient ==== The first reference to the history of the country as a shrine is in the aforementioned article "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}})'Shoku Nihongi' article dated February 22, 702. In , it is said that the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime were separated."Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , seven {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (private houses attached to the shrine) were granted to ``Oyatsuhime Shrine'' in Kii Province {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Related, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794}}) "{{ill|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542}}" is Kii Province {{ill|lt=Nakusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} ``Oya-go'' is listed twice in , but one of these is ``Oya-Kobe'', which means our Kobe, and this Kobe is thought to have been distributed around Udamori. As {{ill|lt=Divine|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), he was elevated to the rank of Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) along with Itagesojin and Tomatsuhimejin.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} |Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa County|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} is listed as "Oya Tsuhime Shrine" Meishin University It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Niinamatsuri|Q11516161}} and {{ill||lt=Ninamesai|UNKNOWN}}, it is written that it was deposited in {{ill|lt=notebook|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}}{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. In ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Oya Okami, the fourth rank of Jushii,'' but it is different from Itakeso Okami, who rose to Shoichii (Senior First Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.Company biography, in April of {{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1088|Q19463|WD=Q19463}}), on the occasion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Horikawa|Q464267|WD=Q464267}}'s visit to {{ill||lt=Kumano|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill||lt=hohei|UNKNOWN}}, and in the first year of {{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} ({{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}}), there were {{ill|lt=Kanda|Q10855080|WD=Q10855080}} of 18{{ill||lt=machibu|UNKNOWN}} (approximately 22 hectares) and 5{{ill|lt=machi (Unit)#Unit of Length|Q1185752|WD=Q1185752|lt=Town}} There is a similar description in 'Ki Province Famous Places Zue' that it prospered due to the donation of {{ill||lt=shrine land|UNKNOWN}} on all four sides (approximately 30 hectares). However, there is no other record of Emperor Horikawa's visit to Kumano. . ==== After the Middle Ages ==== According to the shrine biography , it declined after suffering from the {{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} uprising (first half of the 16th century) and the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} military fire ({{ill|lt=Kishū Conquest#Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack}}). According to "Kanbunki" cited in "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}", in the early modern period it had two towns of Kanda (approximately 2 hectares). Although this Kanda existed until after the war, it is said to have disappeared due to {{ill|lt=agrarian reform|Q208128|WD=Q208128}} in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}21 ({{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610}}). group="Note">However, in Kanbunki, it is written in the past tense, ``existed until recent times (omitted).'' . In addition, the same article mentions that the area around our company, approximately 500 {{ill|lt=stone (unit)|Q855048|WD=Q855048|lt=stone}}, is called the ``Kami no Ki'' and is thought to be the remains of a vast shrine complex from ancient times, but the details are not clear. In "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}," the company at the time was the birthplace deity of the two villages of Udamori and Kitano, but it was originally considered to have been the birthplace deity of the entire Hirata Manor to which these two villages belonged, and it is assumed that due to the separation of the Konishi, Nishitai, and Kita villages due to a conflict within the manor, only the two villages became the birthplace.In April of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} |Q134917284}}, and in 1880|Q7806}}, it was promoted to {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. Also, in 1911|Q2076}}, the Soja Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi in the northern part of Wakayama City was {{ill|lt=combined enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}} (described later). After the war, it belonged to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from the 8th grade of the junior fifth rank to the 8th grade of the lower rank of lower ranks (Junior 4th rank and lower rank of 8th rank) (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Oya Tsuhime no Kami''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii? (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Mecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Oya Okami''. It is said to be an error in writing ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper.'' === About Soja Shrine, a joint shrine === Oyatsuhime Shrine is the Sosha Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi, Kita Aza, Wakayama City, which was enshrined in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}44 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}). This Soja Shrine is a shrine that was also called ``Soja Daimyojin'' in early modern times, but the deity it enshrines is not clear (in ``Ki Meisho Zue'' it is said that {{ill||lt=Nippon Takeru|UNKNOWN}} is enshrined) {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. {{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}) "Nanki Shrine Record" and its subsequent "Ki Meisho Zue" indicate that the shrine name and enshrinement location is "Soja" in Kii. |Q1629088}} is close to the estimated location (present-day Fuchu, Wakayama City), so this Soja Shrine is believed to be {{ill|lt=Soja|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} in Kii Province.{{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. On the other hand, ``Kii Zoku Fudoki'' denies this and states that the shrine that was enshrined in Nezuku Valley in Taisho Ochiai Village (present-day Ochiai, Wakayama City) was transferred from the Sōron of the Shōchu period {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. === Precincts === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine building]] As for the shrine building, the central main hall has an Oji-zukuri copper plate roof, {{ill|lt=facing worship|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970}} is {{ill|lt=kara gable|Q977293|WD=Q977293|lt=kenkara gable}}, and {{jal {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}} where {{ill||lt=Chigi|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill||lt=Katsuoki|UNKNOWN}} are placed in the ridge of ink|Shinsha}}. To keep out the fog, all four sides of the shrine are covered with wooden walls. The side shrines on the left and right are also small-sized {{ill|lt=Nagazuzukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} with copper plate roofing and have chigi and bonito trees {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}}. However, the Chigi of the left wakimiya (Isotake no Mikoto) is a soto-kege, and the chigi of the right wakimiya (Miyatomatohime no Mikoto) is an inward-kege. === Setsusha === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|小箠]] '''Precincts end shrine''' * Small shrine '''Outside shrine''' * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitano, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|12.6|N|135|14|41.1|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Outside shrine: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine}}) === Ritual organization === === Shinto priest === The priesthood of Oyatsuhime Shrine was held for {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769}}) It is said that the Tsuchiya clan ({{ill|lt=real surname|Q11520116|WD=Q11520116}}: Fujiwara clan) has been hereditary since around 1987|1987|p=46-47}}. However, in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue," when it was compiled in the late Edo period, the Shinto priest was the Mori clan, who claimed to be a descendant of Oyahiko no Mikoto, and the current generation is the 83rd generation from Oyahiko no Mikoto. === Parishioners === Oyatsuhime Shrine is said to be the local deity of six districts, including Udamori, where it is enshrined, Hironishi, Kitano, Nango, Nishitai, and Kita. |Q11454763}} was formed, but currently it only remains in the Nagaho district. According to "Kii Zoku Fudoki", {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}}, {{ill|lt=sarugaku|Q1204318|WD=Q1204318}} and {{ill|lt=dengaku|Q3022455|WD=Q3022455}} were once held as festivals on June 1st and September 21st (both according to the lunar calendar). The same book also states that the priests of Itae-so Shrine serve during the festivals held on the last day of October and November 16th (both according to the lunar calendar), and that in October they serve food such as red bean rice, persimmon mochi, fish, and sake. It is said that a {{ill|lt=portable shrine|Q979940|WD=Q979940}} from Itaukeso Shrine was {{ill|lt=transferred|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919}} to our company in November. === Local information === Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} ``Ooyatsuhime Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}} Udamori. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God ===
'''Abbreviated genealogy of the three Ita-kei gods'''

'''Main enshrined deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Ooyatsuhime Mikoto) '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Wakimiya: {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami}} (Itakeru Mikoto) *: Oyatsuhime no Mikoto's older brother deity, {{ill|lt=Ita-Kiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} enshrined deity. * Right side shrine: {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Miyatomatohime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto) *: The sister deity of Oyatsu Hime no Mikoto, and the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Toumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}}.As the company name suggests, our company is said to be a shrine dedicated to '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime}}''' (Oyatsuhime no Mikoto). Regarding Ooyatsuhime, in the 5th book of the ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' Jindaiki's Treasure Sword Appearance Stage (8th Stage of the First Jindai Period), it is described that she is the daughter god of {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} (Susanoo). Also, the older brother god of Ooyatsuhime is {{ill|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the younger sister god is {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Sakutsuhime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsu Both gods are currently enshrined at {{ill|lt=Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Tomatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}} (3 Ronsha shrines), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree seeds to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itae Sosanjin'''' {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Furthermore, in the Jigihonki, ``{{ill|lt=Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}}'', these three gods are said to be the gods enshrined by {{ill|lt=Kiokunizo|Q11604953|WD=Q11604953}}.According to the shrine's legend, there was once a vast forest in the area around our company, and the wood was used to build houses, so it was given the divine name of ``Oya.'' According to "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}", the name of the enshrined place, "Udamori", came from the company's shrine complex.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. === History === === Founding ===Founding date is unknown. Shrine biography"Shrine Furyu" ({{Harvtxt|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=46-47}}). According to .94|N|135|12|4.67|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|Location|name=Maezen Shrine/Kokake Shrine (first enshrined place?①)}}) It was originally enshrined in the ground, but in {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, the site was given to Ryojingu Shrine in 1616 and renamed to the ``Boar Forest'' in Shandong (Itaikeso, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|1 1|58.42|N|135|15|14.38|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Boar Forest (former enshrined place?②)}})Currently, Sansei Shrine, a shrine outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}}, is located in Ino no Mori. {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. After that, "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" written by {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} In the 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oyatsuhime, and Tomatuhime were transferred from Ino-no-Mori to the old shrine north of the current shrine site (Kitano-no-miya, Wakayama City). Mount Hara, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|17.9|N|135|15|2.4|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Old palace (former enshrined place or ③)}}) There are currently no remains of the old palace. , and later relocated to its current location.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}In the Kii Famous Places Zue, ``Mt. Mihara'' is written as ``Mt. Miharayama'', and there is a different legend that it is the place where the three Ita-kami deities descended from the heavens with the seeds of wood, and from there they were divided into different shrines. .Also, "Oyatsu Hime-sha Engi" cited in "Zoku Fudoki" "Kii Zoku Fudoki". In , I will post a separate story that states that the three Itaige deities chose their respective places to be enshrined from Tenuki Bridge (bridge of the heavenly world) and went to various places {{ill|lt=Amakudari|Q480175|WD=Q480175|lt=Amakudari}}, but Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto chose Hirata-go, descended there, ran a palace there, and was enshrined there. Although its relationship with our company is unknown, to the east of the shrine there is the Udamori Ruins, which is thought to be a {{ill|lt=ring-moat village|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739|lt=ring-moat village ruins}} from the middle of the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}}. === Outline === ==== Ancient ==== The first reference to the history of the country as a shrine is in the aforementioned article "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}})'Shoku Nihongi' article dated February 22, 702. In , it is said that the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime were separated."Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , seven {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (private houses attached to the shrine) were granted to ``Oyatsuhime Shrine'' in Kii Province {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Related, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794}}) "{{ill|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542}}" is Kii Province {{ill|lt=Nakusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} ``Oya-go'' is listed twice in , but one of these is ``Oya-Kobe'', which means our Kobe, and this Kobe is thought to have been distributed around Udamori. As {{ill|lt=Divine|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), he was elevated to the rank of Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) along with Itagesojin and Tomatsuhimejin.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} |Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa County|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} is listed as "Oya Tsuhime Shrine" Meishin University It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Niinamatsuri|Q11516161}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninamesai|UNKNOWN}}, it is written that it was deposited in {{ill|lt=notebook|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}}{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. In ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Oya Okami, the fourth rank of Jushii,'' but it is different from Itakeso Okami, who rose to Shoichii (Senior First Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.Company biography, in April of {{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1088|Q19463|WD=Q19463}}), on the occasion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Horikawa|Q464267|WD=Q464267}}'s visit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hohei|UNKNOWN}}, and in the first year of {{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} ({{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}}), there were {{ill|lt=Kanda|Q10855080|WD=Q10855080}} of 18{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=machibu|UNKNOWN}} (approximately 22 hectares) and 5{{ill|lt=machi (Unit)#Unit of Length|Q1185752|WD=Q1185752|lt=Town}} There is a similar description in 'Ki Province Famous Places Zue' that it prospered due to the donation of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine land|UNKNOWN}} on all four sides (approximately 30 hectares). However, there is no other record of Emperor Horikawa's visit to Kumano. . ==== After the Middle Ages ==== According to the shrine biography , it declined after suffering from the {{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} uprising (first half of the 16th century) and the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} military fire ({{ill|lt=Kishū Conquest#Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack}}). According to "Kanbunki" cited in "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}", in the early modern period it had two towns of Kanda (approximately 2 hectares). Although this Kanda existed until after the war, it is said to have disappeared due to {{ill|lt=agrarian reform|Q208128|WD=Q208128}} in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}21 ({{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610}}). group="Note">However, in Kanbunki, it is written in the past tense, ``existed until recent times (omitted).'' . In addition, the same article mentions that the area around our company, approximately 500 {{ill|lt=stone (unit)|Q855048|WD=Q855048|lt=stone}}, is called the ``Kami no Ki'' and is thought to be the remains of a vast shrine complex from ancient times, but the details are not clear. In "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}," the company at the time was the birthplace deity of the two villages of Udamori and Kitano, but it was originally considered to have been the birthplace deity of the entire Hirata Manor to which these two villages belonged, and it is assumed that due to the separation of the Konishi, Nishitai, and Kita villages due to a conflict within the manor, only the two villages became the birthplace.In April of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} |Q134917284}}, and in 1880|Q7806}}, it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. Also, in 1911|Q2076}}, the Soja Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi in the northern part of Wakayama City was {{ill|lt=combined enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}} (described later). After the war, it belonged to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from the 8th grade of the junior fifth rank to the 8th grade of the lower rank of lower ranks (Junior 4th rank and lower rank of 8th rank) (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Oya Tsuhime no Kami''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii? (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Mecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Oya Okami''. It is said to be an error in writing ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper.'' === About Soja Shrine, a joint shrine === Oyatsuhime Shrine is the Sosha Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi, Kita Aza, Wakayama City, which was enshrined in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}44 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}). This Soja Shrine is a shrine that was also called ``Soja Daimyojin'' in early modern times, but the deity it enshrines is not clear (in ``Ki Meisho Zue'' it is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nippon Takeru|UNKNOWN}} is enshrined) {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. {{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}) "Nanki Shrine Record" and its subsequent "Ki Meisho Zue" indicate that the shrine name and enshrinement location is "Soja" in Kii. |Q1629088}} is close to the estimated location (present-day Fuchu, Wakayama City), so this Soja Shrine is believed to be {{ill|lt=Soja|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} in Kii Province.{{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. On the other hand, ``Kii Zoku Fudoki'' denies this and states that the shrine that was enshrined in Nezuku Valley in Taisho Ochiai Village (present-day Ochiai, Wakayama City) was transferred from the Sōron of the Shōchu period {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. === Precincts === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine building]] As for the shrine building, the central main hall has an Oji-zukuri copper plate roof, {{ill|lt=facing worship|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970}} is {{ill|lt=kara gable|Q977293|WD=Q977293|lt=kenkara gable}}, and {{jal {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}} where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chigi|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuoki|UNKNOWN}} are placed in the ridge of ink|Shinsha}}. To keep out the fog, all four sides of the shrine are covered with wooden walls. The side shrines on the left and right are also small-sized {{ill|lt=Nagazuzukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} with copper plate roofing and have chigi and bonito trees {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}}. However, the Chigi of the left wakimiya (Isotake no Mikoto) is a soto-kege, and the chigi of the right wakimiya (Miyatomatohime no Mikoto) is an inward-kege. === Setsusha === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|小箠]] '''Precincts end shrine''' * Small shrine '''Outside shrine''' * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitano, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|12.6|N|135|14|41.1|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Outside shrine: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine}}) === Ritual organization === === Shinto priest === The priesthood of Oyatsuhime Shrine was held for {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769}}) It is said that the Tsuchiya clan ({{ill|lt=real surname|Q11520116|WD=Q11520116}}: Fujiwara clan) has been hereditary since around 1987|1987|p=46-47}}. However, in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue," when it was compiled in the late Edo period, the Shinto priest was the Mori clan, who claimed to be a descendant of Oyahiko no Mikoto, and the current generation is the 83rd generation from Oyahiko no Mikoto. === Parishioners === Oyatsuhime Shrine is said to be the local deity of six districts, including Udamori, where it is enshrined, Hironishi, Kitano, Nango, Nishitai, and Kita. |Q11454763}} was formed, but currently it only remains in the Nagaho district. According to "Kii Zoku Fudoki", {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}}, {{ill|lt=sarugaku|Q1204318|WD=Q1204318}} and {{ill|lt=dengaku|Q3022455|WD=Q3022455}} were once held as festivals on June 1st and September 21st (both according to the lunar calendar). The same book also states that the priests of Itae-so Shrine serve during the festivals held on the last day of October and November 16th (both according to the lunar calendar), and that in October they serve food such as red bean rice, persimmon mochi, fish, and sake. It is said that a {{ill|lt=portable shrine|Q979940|WD=Q979940}} from Itaukeso Shrine was {{ill|lt=transferred|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919}} to our company in November. === Local information === Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} ``Ooyatsuhime Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}} Udamori. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God ===

'''Main enshrined deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Ooyatsuhime Mikoto) '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Wakimiya: {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami}} (Itakeru Mikoto) *: Oyatsuhime no Mikoto's older brother deity, {{ill|lt=Ita-Kiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} enshrined deity. * Right side shrine: {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Miyatomatohime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto) *: The sister deity of Oyatsu Hime no Mikoto, and the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Toumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}}.As the company name suggests, our company is said to be a shrine dedicated to '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime}}''' (Oyatsuhime no Mikoto). Regarding Ooyatsuhime, in the 5th book of the ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' Jindaiki's Treasure Sword Appearance Stage (8th Stage of the First Jindai Period), it is described that she is the daughter god of {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} (Susanoo). Also, the older brother god of Ooyatsuhime is {{ill|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the younger sister god is {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Sakutsuhime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsu Both gods are currently enshrined at {{ill|lt=Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Tomatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}} (3 Ronsha shrines), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree seeds to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itae Sosanjin'''' {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Furthermore, in the Jigihonki, ``{{ill|lt=Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}}'', these three gods are said to be the gods enshrined by {{ill|lt=Kiokunizo|Q11604953|WD=Q11604953}}.According to the shrine's legend, there was once a vast forest in the area around our company, and the wood was used to build houses, so it was given the divine name of ``Oya.'' According to "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}", the name of the enshrined place, "Udamori", came from the company's shrine complex.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. === History === === Founding ===Founding date is unknown. Shrine biography"Shrine Furyu" ({{Harvtxt|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=46-47}}). According to .94|N|135|12|4.67|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|Location|name=Maezen Shrine/Kokake Shrine (first enshrined place?①)}}) It was originally enshrined in the ground, but in {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, the site was given to Ryojingu Shrine in 1616 and renamed to the ``Boar Forest'' in Shandong (Itaikeso, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|1 1|58.42|N|135|15|14.38|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Boar Forest (former enshrined place?②)}})Currently, Sansei Shrine, a shrine outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}}, is located in Ino no Mori. {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. After that, "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" written by {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} In the 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oyatsuhime, and Tomatuhime were transferred from Ino-no-Mori to the old shrine north of the current shrine site (Kitano-no-miya, Wakayama City). Mount Hara, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|17.9|N|135|15|2.4|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Old palace (former enshrined place or ③)}}) There are currently no remains of the old palace. , and later relocated to its current location.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}In the Kii Famous Places Zue, ``Mt. Mihara'' is written as ``Mt. Miharayama'', and there is a different legend that it is the place where the three Ita-kami deities descended from the heavens with the seeds of wood, and from there they were divided into different shrines. .Also, "Oyatsu Hime-sha Engi" cited in "Zoku Fudoki" "Kii Zoku Fudoki". In , I will post a separate story that states that the three Itaige deities chose their respective places to be enshrined from Tenuki Bridge (bridge of the heavenly world) and went to various places {{ill|lt=Amakudari|Q480175|WD=Q480175|lt=Amakudari}}, but Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto chose Hirata-go, descended there, ran a palace there, and was enshrined there. Although its relationship with our company is unknown, to the east of the shrine there is the Udamori Ruins, which is thought to be a {{ill|lt=ring-moat village|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739|lt=ring-moat village ruins}} from the middle of the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}}. === Outline === ==== Ancient ==== The first reference to the history of the country as a shrine is in the aforementioned article "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}})'Shoku Nihongi' article dated February 22, 702. In , it is said that the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime were separated."Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , seven {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (private houses attached to the shrine) were granted to ``Oyatsuhime Shrine'' in Kii Province {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Related, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794}}) "{{ill|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542}}" is Kii Province {{ill|lt=Nakusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} ``Oya-go'' is listed twice in , but one of these is ``Oya-Kobe'', which means our Kobe, and this Kobe is thought to have been distributed around Udamori. As {{ill|lt=Divine|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), he was elevated to the rank of Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) along with Itagesojin and Tomatsuhimejin.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} |Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa County|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} is listed as "Oya Tsuhime Shrine" Meishin University It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Niinamatsuri|Q11516161}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninamesai|1=UNKNOWN}}, it is written that it was deposited in {{ill|lt=notebook|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}}{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. In ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Oya Okami, the fourth rank of Jushii,'' but it is different from Itakeso Okami, who rose to Shoichii (Senior First Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.Company biography, in April of {{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1088|Q19463|WD=Q19463}}), on the occasion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Horikawa|Q464267|WD=Q464267}}'s visit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hohei|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in the first year of {{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} ({{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}}), there were {{ill|lt=Kanda|Q10855080|WD=Q10855080}} of 18{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=machibu|1=UNKNOWN}} (approximately 22 hectares) and 5{{ill|lt=machi (Unit)#Unit of Length|Q1185752|WD=Q1185752|lt=Town}} There is a similar description in 'Ki Province Famous Places Zue' that it prospered due to the donation of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine land|1=UNKNOWN}} on all four sides (approximately 30 hectares). However, there is no other record of Emperor Horikawa's visit to Kumano. . ==== After the Middle Ages ==== According to the shrine biography , it declined after suffering from the {{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} uprising (first half of the 16th century) and the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} military fire ({{ill|lt=Kishū Conquest#Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack}}). According to "Kanbunki" cited in "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}", in the early modern period it had two towns of Kanda (approximately 2 hectares). Although this Kanda existed until after the war, it is said to have disappeared due to {{ill|lt=agrarian reform|Q208128|WD=Q208128}} in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}21 ({{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610}}). group="Note">However, in Kanbunki, it is written in the past tense, ``existed until recent times (omitted).'' . In addition, the same article mentions that the area around our company, approximately 500 {{ill|lt=stone (unit)|Q855048|WD=Q855048|lt=stone}}, is called the ``Kami no Ki'' and is thought to be the remains of a vast shrine complex from ancient times, but the details are not clear. In "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}," the company at the time was the birthplace deity of the two villages of Udamori and Kitano, but it was originally considered to have been the birthplace deity of the entire Hirata Manor to which these two villages belonged, and it is assumed that due to the separation of the Konishi, Nishitai, and Kita villages due to a conflict within the manor, only the two villages became the birthplace.In April of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} |Q134917284}}, and in 1880|Q7806}}, it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. Also, in 1911|Q2076}}, the Soja Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi in the northern part of Wakayama City was {{ill|lt=combined enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}} (described later). After the war, it belonged to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from the 8th grade of the junior fifth rank to the 8th grade of the lower rank of lower ranks (Junior 4th rank and lower rank of 8th rank) (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Oya Tsuhime no Kami''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii? (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Mecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Oya Okami''. It is said to be an error in writing ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper.'' === About Soja Shrine, a joint shrine === Oyatsuhime Shrine is the Sosha Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi, Kita Aza, Wakayama City, which was enshrined in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}44 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}). This Soja Shrine is a shrine that was also called ``Soja Daimyojin'' in early modern times, but the deity it enshrines is not clear (in ``Ki Meisho Zue'' it is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nippon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined) {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. {{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}) "Nanki Shrine Record" and its subsequent "Ki Meisho Zue" indicate that the shrine name and enshrinement location is "Soja" in Kii. |Q1629088}} is close to the estimated location (present-day Fuchu, Wakayama City), so this Soja Shrine is believed to be {{ill|lt=Soja|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} in Kii Province.{{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. On the other hand, ``Kii Zoku Fudoki'' denies this and states that the shrine that was enshrined in Nezuku Valley in Taisho Ochiai Village (present-day Ochiai, Wakayama City) was transferred from the Sōron of the Shōchu period {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. === Precincts === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine building]] As for the shrine building, the central main hall has an Oji-zukuri copper plate roof, {{ill|lt=facing worship|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970}} is {{ill|lt=kara gable|Q977293|WD=Q977293|lt=kenkara gable}}, and {{jal {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}} where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chigi|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuoki|1=UNKNOWN}} are placed in the ridge of ink|Shinsha}}. To keep out the fog, all four sides of the shrine are covered with wooden walls. The side shrines on the left and right are also small-sized {{ill|lt=Nagazuzukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} with copper plate roofing and have chigi and bonito trees {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}}. However, the Chigi of the left wakimiya (Isotake no Mikoto) is a soto-kege, and the chigi of the right wakimiya (Miyatomatohime no Mikoto) is an inward-kege. === Setsusha === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|小箠]] '''Precincts end shrine''' * Small shrine '''Outside shrine''' * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitano, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|12.6|N|135|14|41.1|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Outside shrine: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine}}) === Ritual organization === === Shinto priest === The priesthood of Oyatsuhime Shrine was held for {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769}}) It is said that the Tsuchiya clan ({{ill|lt=real surname|Q11520116|WD=Q11520116}}: Fujiwara clan) has been hereditary since around 1987|1987|p=46-47}}. However, in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue," when it was compiled in the late Edo period, the Shinto priest was the Mori clan, who claimed to be a descendant of Oyahiko no Mikoto, and the current generation is the 83rd generation from Oyahiko no Mikoto. === Parishioners === Oyatsuhime Shrine is said to be the local deity of six districts, including Udamori, where it is enshrined, Hironishi, Kitano, Nango, Nishitai, and Kita. |Q11454763}} was formed, but currently it only remains in the Nagaho district. According to "Kii Zoku Fudoki", {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}}, {{ill|lt=sarugaku|Q1204318|WD=Q1204318}} and {{ill|lt=dengaku|Q3022455|WD=Q3022455}} were once held as festivals on June 1st and September 21st (both according to the lunar calendar). The same book also states that the priests of Itae-so Shrine serve during the festivals held on the last day of October and November 16th (both according to the lunar calendar), and that in October they serve food such as red bean rice, persimmon mochi, fish, and sake. It is said that a {{ill|lt=portable shrine|Q979940|WD=Q979940}} from Itaukeso Shrine was {{ill|lt=transferred|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919}} to our company in November. === Local information === Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} ``Ooyatsuhime Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}} Udamori. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God ===

'''Main enshrined deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Ooyatsuhime Mikoto) '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Wakimiya: {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami}} (Itakeru Mikoto) *: Oyatsuhime no Mikoto's older brother deity, {{ill|lt=Ita-Kiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} enshrined deity. * Right side shrine: {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Miyatomatohime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto) *: The sister deity of Oyatsu Hime no Mikoto, and the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Toumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}}.As the company name suggests, our company is said to be a shrine dedicated to '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime}}''' (Oyatsuhime no Mikoto). Regarding Ooyatsuhime, in the 5th book of the ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' Jindaiki's Treasure Sword Appearance Stage (8th Stage of the First Jindai Period), it is described that she is the daughter god of {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} (Susanoo). Also, the older brother god of Ooyatsuhime is {{ill|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the younger sister god is {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Sakutsuhime no Mikoto}} (Tsumatsu Both gods are currently enshrined at {{ill|lt=Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Tomatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}} (3 Ronsha shrines), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree seeds to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itae Sosanjin'''' {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Furthermore, in the Jigihonki, ``{{ill|lt=Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}}'', these three gods are said to be the gods enshrined by {{ill|lt=Kiokunizo|Q11604953|WD=Q11604953}}.According to the shrine's legend, there was once a vast forest in the area around our company, and the wood was used to build houses, so it was given the divine name of ``Oya.'' According to "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}", the name of the enshrined place, "Udamori", came from the company's shrine complex.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. === History === === Founding ===Founding date is unknown. Shrine biography"Shrine Furyu" ({{Harvtxt|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=46-47}}). According to .94|N|135|12|4.67|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|Location|name=Maezen Shrine/Kokake Shrine (first enshrined place?①)}}) It was originally enshrined in the ground, but in {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, the site was given to Ryojingu Shrine in 1616 and renamed to the ``Boar Forest'' in Shandong (Itaikeso, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|1 1|58.42|N|135|15|14.38|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Boar Forest (former enshrined place?②)}})Currently, Sansei Shrine, a shrine outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}}, is located in Ino no Mori. {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. After that, "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" written by {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} In the 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oyatsuhime, and Tomatuhime were transferred from Ino-no-Mori to the old shrine north of the current shrine site (Kitano-no-miya, Wakayama City). Mount Hara, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|17.9|N|135|15|2.4|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Old palace (former enshrined place or ③)}}) There are currently no remains of the old palace. , and later relocated to its current location.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}In the Kii Famous Places Zue, ``Mt. Mihara'' is written as ``Mt. Miharayama'', and there is a different legend that it is the place where the three Ita-kami deities descended from the heavens with the seeds of wood, and from there they were divided into different shrines. .Also, "Oyatsu Hime-sha Engi" cited in "Zoku Fudoki" "Kii Zoku Fudoki". In , I will post a separate story that states that the three Itaige deities chose their respective places to be enshrined from Tenuki Bridge (bridge of the heavenly world) and went to various places {{ill|lt=Amakudari|Q480175|WD=Q480175|lt=Amakudari}}, but Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto chose Hirata-go, descended there, ran a palace there, and was enshrined there. Although its relationship with our company is unknown, to the east of the shrine there is the Udamori Ruins, which is thought to be a {{ill|lt=ring-moat village|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739|lt=ring-moat village ruins}} from the middle of the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}}. === Outline === ==== Ancient ==== The first reference to the history of the country as a shrine is in the aforementioned article "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}})'Shoku Nihongi' article dated February 22, 702. In , it is said that the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime were separated."Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , seven {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (private houses attached to the shrine) were granted to ``Oyatsuhime Shrine'' in Kii Province {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. Related, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794}}) "{{ill|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542}}" is Kii Province {{ill|lt=Nakusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} ``Oya-go'' is listed twice in , but one of these is ``Oya-Kobe'', which means our Kobe, and this Kobe is thought to have been distributed around Udamori. As {{ill|lt=Divine|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), he was elevated to the rank of Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) along with Itagesojin and Tomatsuhimejin.{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} |Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa County|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} is listed as "Oya Tsuhime Shrine" Meishin University It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Niinamatsuri|Q11516161}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninamesai|1=UNKNOWN}}, it is written that it was deposited in {{ill|lt=notebook|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}}{{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}. In ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Oya Okami, the fourth rank of Jushii,'' but it is different from Itakeso Okami, who rose to Shoichii (Senior First Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=444-445}}.Company biography, in April of {{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1088|Q19463|WD=Q19463}}), on the occasion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Horikawa|Q464267|WD=Q464267}}'s visit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=hohei|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in the first year of {{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} ({{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}}), there were {{ill|lt=Kanda|Q10855080|WD=Q10855080}} of 18{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=machibu|1=UNKNOWN}} (approximately 22 hectares) and 5{{ill|lt=machi (Unit)#Unit of Length|Q1185752|WD=Q1185752|lt=Town}} There is a similar description in 'Ki Province Famous Places Zue' that it prospered due to the donation of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine land|1=UNKNOWN}} on all four sides (approximately 30 hectares). However, there is no other record of Emperor Horikawa's visit to Kumano. . ==== After the Middle Ages ==== According to the shrine biography , it declined after suffering from the {{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} uprising (first half of the 16th century) and the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} military fire ({{ill|lt=Kishū Conquest#Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi's Kishu Attack}}). According to "Kanbunki" cited in "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}", in the early modern period it had two towns of Kanda (approximately 2 hectares). Although this Kanda existed until after the war, it is said to have disappeared due to {{ill|lt=agrarian reform|Q208128|WD=Q208128}} in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}21 ({{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610}}). group="Note">However, in Kanbunki, it is written in the past tense, ``existed until recent times (omitted).'' . In addition, the same article mentions that the area around our company, approximately 500 {{ill|lt=stone (unit)|Q855048|WD=Q855048|lt=stone}}, is called the ``Kami no Ki'' and is thought to be the remains of a vast shrine complex from ancient times, but the details are not clear. In "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}," the company at the time was the birthplace deity of the two villages of Udamori and Kitano, but it was originally considered to have been the birthplace deity of the entire Hirata Manor to which these two villages belonged, and it is assumed that due to the separation of the Konishi, Nishitai, and Kita villages due to a conflict within the manor, only the two villages became the birthplace.In April of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} |Q134917284}}, and in 1880|Q7806}}, it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (corner)|1985}}. Also, in 1911|Q2076}}, the Soja Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi in the northern part of Wakayama City was {{ill|lt=combined enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}} (described later). After the war, it belonged to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from the 8th grade of the junior fifth rank to the 8th grade of the lower rank of lower ranks (Junior 4th rank and lower rank of 8th rank) (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Oya Tsuhime no Kami''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii? (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Mecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Oya Okami''. It is said to be an error in writing ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper.'' === About Soja Shrine, a joint shrine === Oyatsuhime Shrine is the Sosha Shrine (formerly Village Shrine) located in Miyanoshi, Kita Aza, Wakayama City, which was enshrined in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}44 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}). This Soja Shrine is a shrine that was also called ``Soja Daimyojin'' in early modern times, but the deity it enshrines is not clear (in ``Ki Meisho Zue'' it is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nippon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined) {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. {{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}) "Nanki Shrine Record" and its subsequent "Ki Meisho Zue" indicate that the shrine name and enshrinement location is "Soja" in Kii. |Q1629088}} is close to the estimated location (present-day Fuchu, Wakayama City), so this Soja Shrine is believed to be {{ill|lt=Soja|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} in Kii Province.{{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. On the other hand, ``Kii Zoku Fudoki'' denies this and states that the shrine that was enshrined in Nezuku Valley in Taisho Ochiai Village (present-day Ochiai, Wakayama City) was transferred from the Sōron of the Shōchu period {{Sfn|Soja Shrine Ruins (Taira)|1983|p=443}}. === Precincts === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine building]] As for the shrine building, the central main hall has an Oji-zukuri copper plate roof, {{ill|lt=facing worship|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970}} is {{ill|lt=kara gable|Q977293|WD=Q977293|lt=kenkara gable}}, and {{jal {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}} where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chigi|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuoki|1=UNKNOWN}} are placed in the ridge of ink|Shinsha}}. To keep out the fog, all four sides of the shrine are covered with wooden walls. The side shrines on the left and right are also small-sized {{ill|lt=Nagazuzukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} with copper plate roofing and have chigi and bonito trees {{Sfn|Oyatsuhime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=46-47}}. However, the Chigi of the left wakimiya (Isotake no Mikoto) is a soto-kege, and the chigi of the right wakimiya (Miyatomatohime no Mikoto) is an inward-kege. === Setsusha === [[File:Oyatsuhime-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|小箠]] '''Precincts end shrine''' * Small shrine '''Outside shrine''' * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitano, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|16|12.6|N|135|14|41.1|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Outside shrine: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine}}) === Ritual organization === === Shinto priest === The priesthood of Oyatsuhime Shrine was held for {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769}}) It is said that the Tsuchiya clan ({{ill|lt=real surname|Q11520116|WD=Q11520116}}: Fujiwara clan) has been hereditary since around 1987|1987|p=46-47}}. However, in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue," when it was compiled in the late Edo period, the Shinto priest was the Mori clan, who claimed to be a descendant of Oyahiko no Mikoto, and the current generation is the 83rd generation from Oyahiko no Mikoto. === Parishioners === Oyatsuhime Shrine is said to be the local deity of six districts, including Udamori, where it is enshrined, Hironishi, Kitano, Nango, Nishitai, and Kita. |Q11454763}} was formed, but currently it only remains in the Nagaho district. According to "Kii Zoku Fudoki", {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}}, {{ill|lt=sarugaku|Q1204318|WD=Q1204318}} and {{ill|lt=dengaku|Q3022455|WD=Q3022455}} were once held as festivals on June 1st and September 21st (both according to the lunar calendar). The same book also states that the priests of Itae-so Shrine serve during the festivals held on the last day of October and November 16th (both according to the lunar calendar), and that in October they serve food such as red bean rice, persimmon mochi, fish, and sake. It is said that a {{ill|lt=portable shrine|Q979940|WD=Q979940}} from Itaukeso Shrine was {{ill|lt=transferred|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919}} to our company in November. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}Udamori 59 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway
